project: LatentLap-AI Dashboard — Circuit Car Animation Fixes
description: Two bug fixes to SilverstoneCircuit.tsx. Bug 1: car stops moving at lap 29 because totalLaps uses allLaps.length (count of predicted laps, e.g. 29) instead of the max lap number (52). At lap == count the progress formula clamps to 1.0 and the car freezes. Bug 2: car is always drawn vertical regardless of track direction — the transform only translates, never rotates. Fix by computing the path tangent and rotating the car to face its direction of travel. File to edit: dashboard/app/components/SilverstoneCircuit.tsx

userStories.title: Fix circuit car stops moving at lap 29
userStories.description: As a user scrubbing through a race, I want the car marker to keep moving along the Silverstone circuit for every lap until the final lap of the race, not freeze partway through.
acceptanceCriteria: In dashboard/app/components/SilverstoneCircuit.tsx, replace 'const totalLaps = Math.max(allLaps.length, 1)' with a variable that holds the MAX lap number in allLaps, e.g. 'const maxLapNum = allLaps.length > 0 ? allLaps[allLaps.length - 1].lap_number : 1'
Pass maxLapNum (not allLaps.length) to lapToCircuitProgress(currentLap, maxLapNum)
Update the useEffect dependency array to reference maxLapNum instead of totalLaps if needed
For 2025_NOR data (allLaps.length=29 but max lap_number=52): at currentLap=30 progress should be (30-1)/(52-1) ≈ 0.569, NOT clamped to 1.0
Commit with message: 'fix: US-001 - SilverstoneCircuit car stops at lap 29 — use max lap_number not allLaps.length'
userStories.notes: Root cause: allLaps is the subset of laps that have predictions (e.g. 29 for 2025 NOR), but lap numbers go up to 52. Formula (lap-1)/(count-1) reaches 1.0 at lap==count, then clamps. Fix: use Math.max(...allLaps.map(l => l.lap_number)) or allLaps[allLaps.length-1].lap_number (already sorted) as the denominator.

userStories.title: Car faces forward along the track at every position
userStories.description: As a user I want the F1 car marker to always face the direction it is driving along the Silverstone circuit, not always point straight up regardless of the track direction.
acceptanceCriteria: In dashboard/app/components/SilverstoneCircuit.tsx, inside the useEffect that moves the car, compute the path tangent direction after getting the current point
Introduce 'const dist = progress * totalLength' and use 'pathEl.getPointAtLength(dist)' for the current point
Sample a second point slightly ahead: 'const delta = Math.min(2, totalLength * 0.01); const point2 = pathEl.getPointAtLength(Math.min(dist + delta, totalLength - 0.001))'
Compute direction vector: 'const dx = point2.x - point.x; const dy = point2.y - point.y'
Compute angle: 'const angleDeg = Math.atan2(dx, -dy) * (180 / Math.PI)' — this formula is correct because the car SVG is drawn nose-up (pointing toward lower y), so 0 degrees = nose up = -Y direction

Replace the existing carEl.setAttribute transform with: carEl.setAttribute('transform', `translate(${point.x.toFixed(2)}, ${point.y.toFixed(2)}) rotate(${angleDeg.toFixed(1)}) translate(-6, -11)`) — this centers on the path point, rotates, then offsets by car center
Commit with message: 'fix: US-002 - SilverstoneCircuit car faces forward along track — compute path tangent rotation'
userStories.notes: The car SVG is drawn with nose at y~0 and tail at y~21, center at ~(6,11). The SVG 'up' direction (-Y) is the car's forward direction. Math.atan2(dx, -dy) gives the rotation angle such that the car nose points in the direction (dx,dy) of travel. SVG transform 'translate(px,py) rotate(angle) translate(-6,-11)' applies: first shift car center to origin, then rotate, then move to path point.
